title: ptelea angustifolia var. cognata
intro: Ptelea angustifolia var. cognata, also known as the narrow-leaved prickly ash, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its unique, narrow leaves and woody stems. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Ptelea angustifolia var. cognata include relief from digestive issues, such as nausea and diarrhea, as well as its potential to reduce pain and fever. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the leaves or roots in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's extracts in capsule or tincture form.
